title: Power Integrations Earnings Call Shows Momentum Building
text: Power Integrations reported encouraging Q1 2026 results showing early recovery from a cyclical downturn. Industrial segment continued to show strong momentum with 23% year-over-year growth and 15% sequential growth. Financial performance demonstrated stability with non-GAAP gross margin of 53.5% and operating margin reaching 11.7%, up 200 basis points sequentially. Revenue of $108.3 million, up 3% year-over-year and 5% sequentially, signals a return to growth momentum. Data center opportunity emerging as a major growth driver, with management estimating serviceable addressable market to exceed $1 billion by 2030. Strategic collaboration with NVIDIA on high-voltage GaN devices positions the company well for the AI data center buildout. Automotive segment progressing with engagement with 17 of the top 20 EV manufacturers, targeting $100 million revenue by 2029. Management provided Q2 guidance of $115-120 million in revenue, suggesting continued momentum into the second quarter. The earnings call highlighted three strategic priorities: improving customer alignment in product development, streamlining the product pipeline for faster time-to-market, and driving operational efficiency.
